title: Operator-useful Lead Cards and resilient property research
originalPrompt: Fix the lead cards ASAP. The active board must not duplicate one opportunity across parcel verification, seller discovery, and researching rows. Add an operator-controlled delete that preserves recoverability. A new lead may contain any partial combination of seller-provided address, APN/parcel ID, county, city, state, contact details, and notes. It must be a full investigative intake, not a task delegator: normalize APN formatting without changing digits, resolve incomplete locations through reasonable online, county, GIS, assessor, recorder, and browser search paths, use authenticated LandPortal browser access as a high-value research workspace but never as a canonical authority or stopping point, then continue to county/deed, Zillow, and Redfin research. LandPortal API/MCP and paid actions remain forbidden. The Lead Card must visibly show actual parcel/satellite/street/comps-map evidence, selected sold comps, county facts, deed artifact plus owner/easement/restriction review, market pulse, land characteristics, honest score/strategy/valuation gates, and one obvious discovery-call report. Unsupported data must be an explicit gap, never a fabricated result. Max and later training are out of scope until this manual-lead-to-usable-discovery-package loop works. Before completion, the builder must visually operate the live localhost app, open a real operating-style Lead Card and isolated QA Lead Card, inspect the evidence and report, exercise trash/delete, refresh/restart, and verify the usable frontend result rather than relying on backend tests or simulations.

workstreams.name: One-card-per-opportunity board and recoverable operator deletion
workstreams.operatorOutcome: The Acquisitions board is a comprehensible Kanban board where each opportunity appears once, card-level blockers do not create duplicate lanes, Lead-to-Deal is a state change, and the owner can trash and restore an unwanted card.
workstreams.requiredDataState: Existing operating records are preserved. Removal from the active board moves a record to recoverable trash and does not delete evidence or documents without a separate confirmed purge.

steps: Open the operating Acquisitions board
Confirm a known opportunity appears in exactly one active lane
Create an isolated QA lead and confirm it appears once
Move it through a lifecycle transition without duplication
Trash it from the card
Confirm it leaves active lanes
Restore it from Trash
Refresh and restart and confirm persistence

failureConditions: one opportunity renders in multiple active lanes
parcel verification creates a separate board card
a card is permanently lost by ordinary delete
operator cannot identify the next business stage

requirements.text: Each opportunity has exactly one active Kanban card; parcel verification, research state, and discovery gaps are visible card-level status, not duplicate board placement.
The board uses owner-comprehensible operating stages and a Lead becoming a Deal remains the same canonical opportunity.
The owner can move a lead to recoverable Trash and restore it; ordinary deletion does not destroy related operating evidence.
Address/APN duplicate candidates are surfaced for review while distinct parcels are never silently merged.

repairHistory.summary: Changed the WS1 QA journey from an invalid exact-one card assertion to stable single lane-container assertions; distinct opportunities remain multiple cards.
repairHistory.regressionCoverage: property-board-ui.test.ts plus operator-qa-runner/governance focused suite (32 tests).

repairHistory.summary: Replaced brittle visible-text matching with stable opportunity-lane-new_lead and opportunity-lane-discovery_ready test IDs.
repairHistory.regressionCoverage: property-board-ui.test.ts verifies owner-facing stage labels and stable lane contracts.

phases.detail: Canonical opportunity board projection with owner-facing business stages and visible duplicate review warnings implemented; existing Trash/Restore preserved.

Live Hono route tests cover one-card invariant, Lead-to-Deal continuity, Trash removal, and Restore.

workstreams.name: Investigative lead intake and multi-path parcel resolution
workstreams.operatorOutcome: A partial free-form lead dump launches an observable research mission that intelligently resolves parcel identity through formatted APN variants and multiple browser/public search paths rather than stopping after one provider fails.
frontendScreens: Lead Workspace research timeline and next action
workstreams.requiredDataState: Raw seller input remains immutable. Original APN strings and every safe formatting candidate remain attributable. Incomplete or contradictory identity never updates canonical parcel facts without corroboration.

steps: Open New Lead
Paste an address/APN/county partial data dump
Submit once
Confirm the original is visible
Confirm candidate identity and research attempt states are visible
Confirm LandPortal is one attempt rather than a terminal gate
Confirm a no-match produces explicit next research paths rather than a false completion
Refresh and restart and confirm the mission state persists

testRequirements: APN separator/decimal normalization without digit mutation
failureConditions: research is only an in-memory task
a provider claim is shown without visible attempt/provenance

requirements.text: A non-empty free-form lead dump launches an observable, durable investigative mission, not only a task record or generic queue entry.
Parcel search preserves seller input and tests safe county-aware APN/address formatting variants before declaring no match.
LandPortal browser research is a preferred enrichment path when useful but a no-match or UI failure triggers other permitted search paths rather than ending research.
The Lead Card visibly reports attempted paths, evidence, conflicts, confidence, unresolved identity, and safe next action.
No LandPortal API, MCP wrapper, paid action, seller contact, offer, or contract action is used.

repairHistory.summary: Rerun the same canonical read-only browser journey with the Windows process-control permission required by AGENTS.md after taskkill access denied
repairHistory.regressionCoverage: Repeat every visible WS2 assertion, refresh persistence, and canonical managed restart persistence against the same durable fixture

phases.detail: Durable investigative missions now persist digit-preserving APN variants and explicit LandPortal-browser, county GIS/assessor/recorder, web, Zillow, and Redfin paths; no provider is terminal.
18 focused WS2 intake, mission, visible workspace, and safety tests passed.

workstreams.name: Visible evidence-backed discovery-call package
workstreams.operatorOutcome: When research succeeds, the owner can open one obvious discovery-call package on the Lead Card and visually inspect parcel maps, comps, deed, property facts, market pulse, and honest strategy readiness. When research is incomplete, the package plainly shows what was attempted and what is still needed.
workstreams.requiredDataState: Existing evidence is preserved and must never be misrepresented as parcel-associated. Each rendered fact, visual, comp, document, and conclusion has visible provenance and confidence.

steps: Open a resolved isolated QA lead and the operating-style 200 Sid Edens card
Open the discovery package
Visually inspect embedded satellite/street/parcel/comps-map evidence
Inspect selected sold comp cards and disclosures
Open the deed artifact and preliminary review
Inspect market pulse and strategy readiness
Confirm incomplete research does not show unsupported value or strategy conclusions
Download or open report output
Refresh and restart and repeat the visual check

failureConditions: evidence is only an invisible backend link
deed is claimed reviewed without an artifact or explicit unavailable state
strategies are generic or blocked without actionable missing evidence
QA passes without opening the live report

requirements.text: The Lead Card exposes one obvious pre-discovery report entry point and visibly renders current parcel-associated evidence instead of opaque links or count-only claims.

The report shows actual research artifacts where available: parcel/satellite/street/terrain/comps-map visuals, selected sold comp cards, county facts, and a deed document or an explicit retrieval gap.
Deed review visibly identifies record owners and preliminary easement/restriction findings with attributable document provenance; it does not substitute for title/legal advice.

Land characteristics, Market Pulse, valuation, offer range, Land Score, and exit strategies are displayed only when their evidence thresholds are met; otherwise the card states the exact gaps and safe next research action.
Final browser verification is performed against the managed operating localhost app, including opening a Lead Card and its report, not only backend output or isolated simulation.

phases.detail: Added one obvious report entry, artifact and comp-card rendering, attributable deed/source sections, explicit retrieval gaps, and evidence-threshold gating

findings.expected: The journey should confirm that the board renders opportunity cards without requiring exactly one total card.
findings.actual: The generic test-id assertion interpreted an unspecified count as exactly one; 23 distinct opportunity cards were rendered.

history.detail: Changed the WS1 QA journey from an invalid exact-one card assertion to stable single lane-container assertions; distinct opportunities remain multiple cards.

steps: Open the live Acquisitions Pipeline
Assert business-stage lanes
findings.expected: The independent journey should recognize the visible business-stage lanes.
findings.actual: The generic text assertion did not recognize the visible lane labels; stable lane test IDs are required.

history.detail: Replaced brittle visible-text matching with stable opportunity-lane-new_lead and opportunity-lane-discovery_ready test IDs.

steps: Run the read-only ws2-investigative-intake-mission journey
Verify all visible intake and research-path assertions
Run the required canonical managed restart persistence step
findings.expected: The durable investigative mission survives the canonical managed restart and remains visible in the live browser.
findings.actual: All browser assertions and refresh persistence passed, but the non-elevated QA child process could not terminate the verified runtime PID: taskkill returned Access denied.
findings.apiOrDbEvidence: The runtime was healthy and repository-associated at preflight; the failure was limited to Windows process-control permission during restart.
